mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments that require efficient orchestration of large language models and intelligent scheduling to handle high-scale traffic. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources to maximize performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface (CLI) to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Install the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.31

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart into the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaling configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step will upgrade the llm-d deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current deployment configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version of llm-d.
> Verify that the pods are running and ready after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or quota limits in your GCP project.

**Fix:**
Check your GCP IAM permissions and ensure you have sufficient quota for load balancers.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if there is a misconfiguration in the application or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the request to the Kubernetes API server timed out.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and ensure the Kubernetes API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud resources before proceeding.
